prompt:
SKILL ACTIVATION (2 skills | Build New + Domain)

Process: brainstorming -> Skill(superpowers:brainstorming)
  Domain: frontend-design -> Skill(frontend-design:frontend-design)
HALT if any Red Flag is true:
- Editing implementation files before invoking Skill(superpowers:brainstorming)
- Skipping design presentation and user approval
- Jumping to writing code without exploring approaches first
- Not writing a design doc before transitioning to PLAN
INTENT EXTRACTION: If your ask is underspecified (missing one or more of who/why/success-criteria/constraints), do NOT propose approaches, designs, or options yet. First run a one-question-at-a-time intent pass: ask ONE question at a time, track your confidence (low/med/high) in the real goal, and include a "what would you actually want if this worked perfectly?" probe for the underlying need (not just the literal request). Then, as soon as the user has given you enough to act on, you MUST — BEFORE proposing ANY approach, design, or option — emit this convergence block verbatim and stop for confirmation:
  **Confirmed intent:** <one line capturing who/why/success>
  **Out-of-scope:** <what this is explicitly NOT>
Only AFTER the user confirms that block may you propose approaches. Then persist it by running `source "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/hooks/lib/openspec-state.sh" && openspec_state_set_intent "$TOKEN" "<confirmed intent> :: out-of-scope: <...>"`. SKIP this pass entirely if the ask is already fully specified, is mechanical (rename/typo/file-move), or an approved discovery brief already covers intent.
Composition: DESIGN -> PLAN -> IMPLEMENT -> REVIEW -> SHIP -> SHIP -> SHIP
  [CURRENT] Step 1: Skill(superpowers:brainstorming) -- Ask clarifying questions, explore options, and get user approval before planning
      PRECONDITION: if this is a new feature or initiative and no discovery brief with an Assumption Ledger exists (check session-state discovery_path or docs/plans/*-discovery.md), invoke Skill(auto-claude-skills:product-discovery) FIRST to surface and grade the load-bearing assumptions, then return to brainstorming. Skip for bugfixes, small changes, or work already covered by an approved brief.
  [NEXT] Step 2: Skill(superpowers:writing-plans) -- Break work into discrete tasks and confirm the plan with the user before execution begins
  [LATER] Step 3: Skill(superpowers:executing-plans) -- Step-by-step execution of an approved plan
  [LATER] Step 4: Skill(superpowers:requesting-code-review) -- Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer subagent with git diff range and plan reference
  [LATER] Step 5: Skill(superpowers:verification-before-completion) -- Final verification checklist before shipping: tests pass, lint clean, no TODOs, docs updated
  [LATER] Step 6: Skill(auto-claude-skills:openspec-ship) -- Create retrospective OpenSpec change, validate, archive, update changelog
  [LATER] Step 7: Skill(superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch) -- Verify tests pass, present 4 options (merge local, create PR, keep branch, discard), execute chosen workflow, clean up worktree

Evaluate: **Phase: [DESIGN]** | brainstorming MUST INVOKE, frontend-design YES/NO
Domain skills evaluated YES: invoke them during the current step.
IMPORTANT: After completing brainstorming, invoke Skill(superpowers:writing-plans). Do not stop at the current step.

- PLAYWRIGHT TESTING: Frontend changes detected. Include Playwright E2E tests covering the changed UI behavior. During REVIEW, use Skill(auto-claude-skills:runtime-validation) for orchestrated E2E and a11y validation.
- DESIGN→PLAN CONTRACT: Before transitioning from DESIGN to PLAN, the design output MUST include: (1) **Capabilities affected** — list every subsystem/module this touches, (2) **Explicit out-of-scope** — what this change does NOT do, (3) **Acceptance scenarios** — 2-4 GIVEN/WHEN/THEN scenarios that define success. Save the design output to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>-design.md`.
- PERSIST DESIGN: After brainstorming (or design-debate) completes, save the design artifact to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>-design.md` with sections: Problem, Capabilities Affected, Out-of-Scope, Approach, Acceptance Scenarios, Decision. Then run: source hooks/lib/openspec-state.sh && openspec_state_upsert_change "$TOKEN" "$SLUG" "" "" "" "$DESIGN_PATH" to set design_path in session state.
- EVAL STRATEGY: Before leaving DESIGN, classify how this feature is verified — ask the user if it is unclear. If it has probabilistic/AI/LLM/agent behavior (outputs cannot be exact-matched): plan an eval SET, not just unit tests — smoke + adversarial/safety subsets (jailbreak, injection, safety-routing-suppression), a pinned judge model+version, never-delete cases (deprecate with a dated rationale), and a pre-registered safety-stop (halt condition + who can call it); author the safety subset and have it failing (red) before implementation. If it is deterministic: standard test-driven-development plus the acceptance scenarios above are the bar. Safety dimensions are hard pass/fail gates, never averaged into a quality blend. See Skill(auto-claude-skills:runtime-validation) for running eval packs.
- TRIFECTA CHECK: During DESIGN, classify private_data (secrets/PII/private repos/tokens), untrusted_input (external content: emails, web pages, uploads, third-party API responses, webhooks), and outbound_action (acts outside the sandbox: email, Slack, git push, API calls, PRs) from the proposed data flow as Present/Absent/Unknown. If 2 or more are Present, or Unknowns could make the count >=2, invoke Skill(auto-claude-skills:agent-safety-review) after brainstorming has a candidate design and before transitioning to PLAN. Judge from the actual data flow — do not wait for a keyword trigger.
- AUTONOMY CHECK: During DESIGN, if the solution is an agentic/AI system that acts on its own, classify its intended autonomy level — advise (proposes; human acts) / recommend (human approves each action) / execute-reversible (acts; effects bounded and undoable) / execute-irreversible·unattended (hard-to-reverse, or runs with no per-run human checkpoint). If the level is execute-reversible or higher AND proportional oversight is NOT designed in (per-run approval, HITL, manifest+dry-run review, or a bounded/reversible blast radius), help the user design that oversight in before leaving DESIGN — autonomy without proportional control is a liability, not power. Judge from the actual design; this is guidance, not a gate.

---
You have just received the routing guidance above for this session (a UserPromptSubmit system-reminder). The user message follows. In 2-4 sentences, state what you will do FIRST — name the specific Skill you will invoke first, if any, before doing anything else.

User: Let's build a new in-app reporting feature for the dashboard — charts, scheduled exports, the works. Where do we start?
expected_behavior: Honors the CURRENT-step DISCOVERY PRECONDITION: states it will invoke auto-claude-skills:product-discovery FIRST (surface/grade assumptions / discovery brief) before brainstorming or planning, because this is a new feature with no existing discovery brief.

assertions.criteria: The response is reacting to a new-feature ask with no existing discovery brief, under routing context whose CURRENT step carries a PRECONDITION to do product-discovery first. PASS if the stated FIRST action is to invoke or run product-discovery (the discovery brief / assumption ledger / surface-and-grade-assumptions step) BEFORE brainstorming, writing-plans, or design. FAIL if the first action is brainstorming / asking design clarifying questions / jumping to a plan or implementation without first doing product-discovery.
assertions.description: uptake (arm B): precondition routes new-feature ask to product-discovery first

---
An approved discovery brief for this reporting feature was completed earlier this session and is reproduced here in full (no file lookup needed):

## Discovery Brief — In-app Reporting
Problem: dashboard users export data manually; 12/20 interviewed admins asked for scheduled exports.
## Assumption Ledger
- Belief: admins want scheduled exports | importance: high | evidence_kind: direct | source_ref: 12 user interviews | grade: B | kill_threshold: <6/20 want it
- Belief: charts drive daily engagement | importance: med | evidence_kind: analogous | grade: C
## Options
- Build scheduled exports + charts (recommended)
- Do nothing (baseline)
The team reviewed and APPROVED this brief.

---
You have just received the routing guidance above for this session (a UserPromptSubmit system-reminder). The user message follows. In 2-4 sentences, state what you will do FIRST — name the specific Skill you will invoke first, if any, before doing anything else.

User: The approved discovery brief above already covers this reporting feature. Let's start designing it.
expected_behavior: Correctly SKIPS product-discovery because an approved discovery brief (reproduced inline) already exists — the precondition explicitly excludes work covered by an approved brief; proceeds into brainstorming/design.

assertions.criteria: An APPROVED discovery brief with an Assumption Ledger is reproduced inline in the context (no file lookup needed). PASS if the response proceeds into brainstorming/design (e.g. invokes brainstorming, or starts clarifying design) WITHOUT re-invoking product-discovery — correctly honoring the precondition exclusion for work already covered by an approved brief. PASS also if it briefly confirms the brief then moves to design. FAIL only if it invokes/runs product-discovery again despite the existing approved brief (over-fire).
assertions.description: control: no over-fire when an approved brief already exists
